arts.sum: Stoke were humiliated by play-off outsiders Norwich at Carrow Road, losing 4-2 against Johannes Thorup's side. Mark Robins' men can at least take comfort from a four-match home unbeaten run, but the...

arts.ttl: The Middlesbrough player who created many of Brian Clough's goals but only after catching the bus
arts.sum: In a time when Boro regularly played a 2-3-5 formation, the pair were part of the ’£50 forward line’, which as well as Clough and McLean, also featured Peacock, Billie Day and Eddie Holliday. Known as Derek throughout his Boro career, McLean would come through the Boro youth ranks and into the first team at the same time as Brian Clough and Alan Peacock.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's transfer 'click' hope and Kelechi Iheanacho Middlesbrough role admission
arts.sum: The boys that have come in are all good players, and they’ve fitted into the group fantastically well. He continued: “There’s no getting away from the fact that it’s been a difficult time for those players to come into the team and the group.

arts.ttl: Stoke City a juggernaut to turn around but I won't throw baby out with bath water
arts.sum: Mark Robins is looking for Stoke City to strike a better balance in and out of possession as they head into back-to-back home games – and get right what they got wrong at Norwich. On Tuesday, it’s an opportunity to get it right and strike that balance and get back to more of the things we were doing, certainly against Swansea, when there were upticks in our outputs out of possession, in possession was a bit better, there was more intent in the game.

arts.ttl: Stoke City injury latest as Mark Robins delivers updates on Wouter Burger and Sam Gallagher
arts.sum: Robins said: “It’s a good opportunity, game 34 in the season and we’re playing catch up a little bit. Robins said: “I think from a physical perspective he’s a really, really strong, powerful player that has still got to learn how to use his strength and power to get the maximum benefit for himself and for us.

arts.ttl: I'm an eternal optimist and I'm refusing to give up on Middlesbrough's season yet
arts.sum: Now on a run of five consecutive defeats, they’ve dropped into the bottom half of the Championship after moving into the top six with their last-ditch win over Hull City on New Year’s Day. Understandably, there’s a lot of frustration for Boro fans right now, and questions are being asked of head coach Michael Carrick after such a horrific slide. Nevertheless, whether it’s conversations about Carrick’s future, Boro team selection or the like, I can’t subscribe to a common thing I’m seeing over and over again from many: ‘Boro’s season is now over’.

arts.ttl: January signing fit again for Middlesbrough at Stoke City but seven players ruled out
arts.sum: Michael Carrick will still be without key loan forward Ben Doak, however, although the Liverpool loanee is hoping to join back in training next week after missing the last four games. Michael Carrick told GazetteLive: “None of the injured lads will be back, but Ryan has recovered from illness over the course of the weekend and he’s back in and around it, which is good.I don’t expect the injured lads to be back for the weekend either, they’re a little bit further away than that.

arts.ttl: Manhoef starts: Stoke City team news and predicted XI to face Middlesbrough
arts.sum: The latest Stoke City team news as Mark Robins' side gear up to face Middlesbrough in the Championship. The post Manhoef starts: Stoke City team news and predicted XI to face Middlesbrough appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Million Manhoef on a year at Stoke City and changing team mindset
arts.sum: It’s 40 games, nine goals, three managers, 40 team-mates, one annoying injury and two battles at the wrong end of the division since the 23-year-old winger joined Stoke City from Vitesse Arnhem mid-way through last season. “It’s hard for the whole team or the whole of Stoke to think the same, of course, but that’s what I’m trying to say to the guys, ‘What if we win 13 games in a row?

arts.ttl: ‘Everyone’s waiting’ – Middlesbrough boss issues update on Liverpool loanee’s injury recovery
arts.sum: Middlesbrough hope Ben Doak can be ‘in and around’ training again next week, Michael Carrick has said. Middlesbrough… The post ‘Everyone’s waiting’ – Middlesbrough boss issues update on Liverpool loanee’s injury recovery appeared first on The72 - Football League News.
